title: Orderly Queue
description:
You are given a string s and an integer k. You can choose one of the first k letters of s and append it at the end of the string..

Return _the lexicographically smallest string you could have after applying the mentioned step any number of moves_.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def longest_distance(n):
    last = -1
    longest = 0
    i = 0
    while n:
        if n & 1:
            if last >= 0:
                longest = max(longest, i - last)
            last = i
        n >>= 1
        i += 1
    return longest
```
    
    The algorithm iterates through the bits of the integer `n` using bitwise operations. It keeps track of the position of the last encountered 1 and calculates the distance between the current 1 and the last 1, updating the longest distance if necessary. If two consecutive 1's are found, it updates the last 1's position with the current position.

1. Initialize `last` to -1 and `longest` to 0.
2. Iterate through the binary representation of `n` (by shifting n right by 1)
   a. If the current bit is 1:
      i. If `last` is not -1, calculate the distance between the current bit position and the last position of 1 and update `longest` with the maximum of current `longest` value and calculated distance.
      ii. Update `last` with the current position i.
3. Return `longest`.
